title: hieracium wettsteinianum subsp. neomalyanum
intro: Hieracium wettsteinianum subsp. neomalyanum, also known as Wettstein's hawkweed, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Hieracium genus, characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and narrow leaves. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Hieracium wettsteinianum subsp. neomalyanum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, or decoctions, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications.
